task_completion.description: Open the eCRM-001 model and the CFD Mesh tool in OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP main window is open showing the aircraft model, and the 'CFD Mesh' dialog box is open.

task_completion.description: Set the Global Max Edge Length to 0.40 m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the CFD Mesh dialog (likely under the 'Global' or 'Setup' tab), the 'Max Edge' or similar global edge length parameter is set to 0.40.

task_completion.description: Add a new refinement source of type 'Box'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Sources' tab of the CFD Mesh dialog, a refinement source is listed and its type is set to 'Box'.

task_completion.description: Configure the Box source parameters according to the specification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Sources' tab for the Box refinement, the parameters are set to: Center X=52.0, Y=0.0, Z=5.0; Extents Length=10.0, Width=18.0, Height=8.0; and Target Edge Length=0.05.
A mesh viewer window appears showing the generated mesh, or there is console/terminal output within OpenVSP indicating that the meshing process is running or has completed.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting mesh in Cart3D (.tri) format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog showing the export of a .tri file named 'eCRM001_refined.tri' to the 'exports' directory, or a file manager/terminal showing this file exists.
A file save dialog saving the model as 'eCRM001_mesh_setup.vsp3', the OpenVSP window title updating to reflect this new filename, or a file manager showing the new .vsp3 file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenVSP GUI to configure the mesh.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the OpenVSP interface and CFD Mesh dialogs, rather than editing the .vsp3 XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the mesh was genuinely generated by OpenVSP.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenVSP meshing console output, progress bars, or the built-in mesh viewer, ensuring the agent didn't just use terminal commands to create a dummy .tri file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the task by copying files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen using 'cp' or 'mv' commands in the terminal to simply rename the original .vsp3 file to the target output name without making the required GUI changes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The refinement specification requires the following exact values in the CFD Mesh tool: Global Max Edge Length = 0.40. Box Center: X=52.0, Y=0.0, Z=5.0. Box Extents: Length=10.0, Width=18.0, Height=8.0. Target Source Edge Length = 0.05. Note: The box center X=52.0 is geometrically outside the aircraft bounding box (due to a known defect in the task spec), so the final generated mesh might not visually show local refinement on the aircraft itself. However, the agent MUST enter these exact parameters into the CFD Mesh Sources tab to receive credit.
